subject: [PATCH v4 2/4] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that since sv_nrpools is either '1' or equal to svc_pool_map.count, it can be removed and only sv_is_pooled used for simplification and clarity in the code.
reviews.raw_body:
I think this patch is good, but there is probably room for further
simplification which is probably best left to a later patch.
In particular, ->sv_nrpools is now either '1' or svc_pool_map.count,
and these possibilities match ->sv_is_pooled.  So we only need one of
those.
I think removing ->sv_nrpools and only using ->sv_is_pooled would
simplify/clarify some of the code.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed NeilBrown's suggestion to simplify the code by removing mode-based switch statements and instead using a pernode path. The author explained that this is not possible because sv_is_pooled cannot be derived from sv_nrpools due to the single-node case, and that converting all accesses of sv_nrpools would require creating more accessors for the svc_pool_map, which could have performance implications.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks. I took a look at that cleanup, but it's not quite the same.

  - unpooled service (lockd, NFS callback): sv_is_pooled=false, sv_nrpools=1
  - pooled service on a single-node host: sv_is_pooled=true, sv_nrpools=1 (pernode degenerates to one pool)
  - pooled service on multi-node: sv_is_pooled=true, sv_nrpools=N>1

So you can't derive sv_is_pooled from sv_nrpools because of the
single-node case.

Worse, it also means that we'd have to create more accessors for the
svc_pool_map. There are several places that access sv_nrpools that
would have to be converted to get it from the map. That may have
performance implications too since the map is accessed during receives.

So this kind of cleanup is doable, but it's a bit messier than it
sounds at first.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch hardcodes the pool mode to pernode, removing other modes such as SVC_POOL_AUTO/GLOBAL/PERCPU/PERNODE. The default behavior on multi-NUMA hosts changes from a single global pool to one pool per NUMA node. This change simplifies the code and improves performance by providing proper thread affinity and NUMA-local memory allocation. The patch also removes deprecated code, including mode selection heuristics and switch statements, and updates documentation to reflect the new behavior.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary:
Reviewer NeilBrown agreed with the patch, but expressed skepticism about removing sv_nrpools and suggested keeping it due to its implication of a reference to svc_pool_map.

Reviewer NeilBrown noted that svc_pool_map is a single global variable and thus does not require dereferencing, making the patch's concern about accessing shared pool_to[] array unnecessary.
reviews.raw_body:
Agree.  We have to keep sv_is_pooled (it implies a reference to
svc_pool_map).  I'm not convinced that we need sv_nrpools.

---

svc_pool_map is a single global that is read-mostly, so no dereference,
easy to predict.
During receive we need to access svc_pool_map anyway to map CPU to POOL.
So the svc_pool_map.npools will have to be pulled into cache anyway.

But if you don't like it, I won't push it on you.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch hardcodes the pool mode to pernode, removing other modes such as SVC_POOL_AUTO/GLOBAL/PERCPU/PERNODE. The default behavior on multi-NUMA hosts changes from a single global pool to one pool per NUMA node. This change simplifies the code and improves performance by providing proper thread affinity and NUMA-local memory allocation. The patch also removes deprecated code, including mode selection heuristics and switch statements, and updates documentation to reflect the new behavior.
